label: Config
info:
Loads system configuration from PostgreSQL on startup.
Sets global.printerList, global.zoneList, global.equipmentList.

label: [Control] Printers
info:
Print control — replaces SysLink + SysComm printer logic.

Flow:
1. Subscribe to PLC tags: DataReady, Print, File_Path per printer
2. When DataReady AND Print both true: send IPP Print-Job directly to printer
3. Monitor port 9100 TCP socket for Epson label-ready status (index 19)
4. Write JobDone + Label_RDY back to PLC
5. Reset when PLC clears Print tag

PLC connection reuses existing eip-connection (c7fb45799993c64c).
Print assets path: PRINT_ASSETS_PATH env var (default /print-assets).

name: Map to printerList
Query Printers
query: SELECT * FROM printers WHERE enabled = true ORDER BY name

name: Set Constants
Cron job on user systemex keeps last 7 days of flows.json
Query Zones
query: SELECT * FROM zones WHERE enabled = true ORDER BY name

name: Map to zoneList
Query Equipment
query: SELECT * FROM equipment WHERE enabled = true ORDER BY name

name: Map to equipmentList
AUTO-CREATE TABLES (runs on every startup, safe to re-run)
info:
Creates required tables if they do not exist.
Safe to run on every startup — IF NOT EXISTS prevents data loss.
Add new tables here as the project grows.
Runs before data queries so a fresh PostgreSQL server is fully provisioned automatically.

name: CREATE printers
query:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS printers (
    id           SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    name         VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    type         VARCHAR(50),
    location     VARCHAR(100),
    ip_address   VARCHAR(15),
    port         INTEGER DEFAULT 9100,
    mqtt_key     VARCHAR(50),
    enabled      BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    config       JSONB DEFAULT '{}'
);

name: CREATE zones
CREATE equipment
query:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS equipment (
    id           SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    name         VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    display_name VARCHAR(100),
    zone         VARCHAR(50),
    enabled      BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    config       JSONB DEFAULT '{}'
);

name: CREATE print_jobs
query:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS print_jobs (
    id           SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    printer      VARCHAR(100),
    file_path    TEXT,
    started_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
    finished_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
    duration_ms  INTEGER,
    result       VARCHAR(20),
    error_msg    TEXT
);

Build Reset OFF
func:
msg.payload = { tags: [{ name: msg.eqName + '.HMI.In.Reset_Rqst', value: false, type: 'BOOL' }] };
return msg;

name: Build Zone Tag List
Build Reset Tag
EQUIPMENT SUBSCRIPTION
info:
Tag list is built dynamically from global.equipmentList (loaded by Config tab from DB on startup).
Each equipment's config JSONB flags determine which PLC tags get subscribed:
  hasPe1          → {EQ}.PE[1].In.Presence
  hasPe2          → {EQ}.PE[2].In.Presence
  hasCurrentStep  → {EQ}.SeqOperation_CurrentStep
  hasMt1          → {EQ}.MT[1].HMI.In.Speed
  hasVg1          → {EQ}.VG[1].In.PSS
  cylinders: N    → {EQ}.CY[1..N].In.RSX / RSR
Fires once on startup. Gate: skipped if PLC_DRIVER != 'eip'.

name: EQUIPMENT RESET
info:
Triggered by MQTT topic equipment/{name}/reset (published by dashboard or alarm logic).
Pulses {EQname}.HMI.In.Reset_Rqst: TRUE → 200ms delay → FALSE via EIP write.
Gate: skipped if PLC_DRIVER != 'eip'.

name: GENERIC TAG OPERATIONS (plc/* MQTT contract)
info:
Three chains handle generic PLC read/write requests from Integration tabs:
  plc/subscribe → dynamic tag subscription → publishes plc/tags/{name}
  plc/write      → writes one or more tags
  plc/pulse      → pulses a tag (true 200ms false)
  plc/toggle     → toggles a tag
All gated: skipped if PLC_DRIVER != 'eip'.

name: Format Status Topic
Format Alarm Payload
Initialize the printers Statuses
Manage Zebra TCP
Update Zebra State
Poll Brother Status
Parse Brother Status
Check RFID Comm
Write Alarm/Warning to PLC
Manage Epson TCP
Parse Epson Status
Write ComOK
Runs once on start. Clears dashboard state, initializes all printer flow states to INITIALIZING, publishes blank retained MQTT alarms so the HMI starts clean.
Polls all EPSON_CW6500 printers every 5s via SNMP (staggered 3/s). Reads hrPrinterStatus (idle/printing) and hrPrinterDetectedErrorState BITS (paper low, ink empty, jam,   etc.). → Shared Output
Maintains persistent TCP connections to all Epson printers port 9100. Detects label ready when response index [19]='1'. Writes JobDone + Label_RDY to PLC on state change. →   PLC Writes link out

Polls IPP Get-Printer-Attributes every 5s (HTTP port 631). Parses printer-state (3=idle, 4=printing, 5=stopped) and scans response for tray alarms (Pas papier B1/B2/B3),   toner-empty, media-jam. → Shared Output
Persistent TCP to port 9100, sends ~HS every 500ms. Parses response indices: 1=paper out, 2=paused, 14=head open, 15=ribbon out, 19=label ready. → Shared Output
TCP connect/timeout to port 9100 every 5s — connectivity check only, no status parsing. Sets ComOK + online state. → Shared Output

Receives printer state from all monitoring groups. Publishes status to MQTT every poll. On change: publishes retained alarm to HMI, writes Alarm/Warning SINT tags to PLC,   writes ComOK BOOL to PLC. → MQTT broker + PLC Writes link out
Update Printer State
Mark Brother Offline
Build PLC Tag List
Process PLC Changes
Build IPP Request
Handle Response
Build ZPL Request
Build Brother PDF
Check Brother Jobs
Handle Brother Job Status
Filter Unchanged Tags
Check Job Timeouts
Write Startup Defaults

On start: subscribes to all printer PLC tags (tag list driven by printerConfig.subscribeTags). Detects rising edge on DataReady+Print (or SendData for RFID) to trigger a print    job. → Route by printer type + PLC Write Pipeline
Builds binary IPP Print-Job request (operation 0x0002), reads ZPL/image file from print assets path. Sends HTTPS POST to ipps://{ip}:631/ipp/print (self-signed cert accepted).    → Handle Response
Reads ZPL file from print assets, sends raw bytes over TCP to port 9100. Used by both ZT421 (label printer) and ZE511R (RFID). → Handle Response

Generates PDF dynamically via pdfkit (landscape, product name + qty + date, font 50). Sends IPP HTTP POST to port 631. Monitors job completion via Get-Jobs every 1s — done   when no job-attributes-tag (0x05) in response. → PLC Write Pipeline

Job timeout: every 30s, resets any print job stuck in PRINTING for >2min (writes Available=true to PLC). Startup defaults: fires once after Config loads, writes safe initial   tag values (ComOK=false, Available=true, etc.) and clears stale job states.
Single entry point for all PLC writes (from Control + Monitor tabs via link node). Filters out tags whose value hasn't changed since last write. Serializes all writes through   one EIP connection.
Validate Print File
Disabled by default. Enable manually to test a printer without PLC. Uses hardcoded printer config — update if IPs change
Log Job End
Update Job Log
query: UPDATE print_jobs SET finished_at=$2, duration_ms=$3, result=$4, error_msg=$5 WHERE id=$1

Log Job Start
Delete Old Logs
query: DELETE FROM print_jobs WHERE started_at < NOW() - ($1 * INTERVAL '1 day')

name: Insert Job Log
query: INSERT INTO print_jobs (printer, equipment, file_path, started_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4) RETURNING id

name: Parse Metrics
Server Monitor
DATA SOURCE
TAG → VARIABLE MAP
info:
Regex rules match incoming PLC tag names and map them to semantic variable names:
  PE[1].In.Presence          → sensorPresence
  PE[2].In.Presence          → sensorPe2Presence
  SeqOperation_CurrentStep   → currentStep
  MT[1].HMI.In.Speed         → speed
  CY[N].In.RSX               → cylinderEntered
  CY[N].In.RSR               → cylinderExited
  VG[1].In.PSS               → suctionCupPresence
To add a new tag type: add a { regex, variable } entry to TAG_VARIABLE_MAP in this function node.

name: OUTPUT
SERVER METRICS POLL
info:
Polls Node-RED's own /metrics endpoint (Prometheus format) every 10 seconds.
Parses key stats: CPU usage, memory (heap used/total), uptime, event loop lag.
Publishes live values to the Server Monitor dashboard page (/dashboard/server).

Metrics source: http://localhost:1880/metrics
Poll interval: configurable in the inject node (default 10s).

name: Alarm to DB
Alarm to WS
TAG HUB — WebSocket + HTTP bridge to PLC via MQTT
info:
External HMI (HMI-Forge or browser) connects via WebSocket /tag-hub.

Subscription flow:
  Client sends {tags:[...]} → plc/subscribe MQTT → Driver tab → eip-subscription
  Driver publishes plc/tags/{name} → forwarded back to all WS clients

Command flow (POST /hmi-forge-command):
  {type:'set_tag',  tag:{name,value}} → plc/write  MQTT → Driver tab
  {type:'pulse_tag',tag:{name}}       → plc/pulse  MQTT → Driver tab
  {type:'toggle_tag',tag:{name}}      → plc/toggle MQTT → Driver tab
  {type:'get_tag'}                    → no-op (reads come via subscription)

All PLC operations handled by the active Driver tab — no PLC nodes here.

name: Parse + Forward to plc/subscribe
Format for WS
Route Command
ALARMS — machine/alarm/* MQTT + dynamic tag subscription
info:
Two sources of alarm data:
1. machine/alarm/* — published by the active Driver tab (PackML alarms)
   Forwarded directly to all connected WebSocket /ws/chemtecAlarms clients.
2. plc/subscribe — dynamic tag subscription for raw alarm tags
   WebSocket client sends {tags:[...]} to subscribe, receives plc/tags/* updates.
3. On WebSocket disconnect — subscription is cleared automatically.

name: Format machine alarm
Parse + Forward
Parse BigInt + Format
Clear on Disconnect
RECIPES — WebSocket subscription + HTTP write/control
info:
WebSocket /ws/chemtecRecipe:
  Client sends {command:'get_recipe'} → tag list built → plc/subscribe
  plc/tags/* updates received → converted to recipe object → sent back to client

HTTP endpoints:
  POST /recipe/set_current_recipe  {lineId, recipe} → plc/write (28 STRING tags per line)
  POST /recipe/start_production                      → plc/pulse  {AR*.Current_Recipe.Start}
  POST /recipe/cancel_production                     → plc/pulse  {AR*.Current_Recipe.Cancel}

Tag names are Chemtec-specific (AR11000/AR12000/AR13000/AR14000.Current_Recipe.*).
Update per project in the function nodes below.

name: Tag Injecter
Convert Tags to Recipe Object
Map Recipe → PLC Tags
Build Start Pulse
Build Cancel Pulse
Map Machine Alarm
Update Printer Row
func:
const p = msg.payload;
  if (!p) return null;
  const printerName = p.printer || p.name;                                                                                                                 
  if (!printerName) return null;                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                             
  const rows = flow.get('dash_printers') || {};                 
  const prev = rows[printerName] || {};                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                           
  const printers = global.get('printerList') || [];
  const order = printers.map(pr => pr.name);
  const printerConfig = printers.find(pr => pr.name === printerName) || {};                                                                                   
  rows[printerName] = {                                                                                                                                    
      name:      printerName,                                                                                                                              
      equipment: p.equipment || prev.equipment || printerConfig.equipment || '',
      location:  p.location  || prev.location  || printerConfig.location  || '',                                                                           
      status:    p.status      || prev.status      || 'UNKNOWN',                                                                                           
      alarm:     p.alarmText   || prev.alarm       || 'None',                                                                                              
      warning:   p.warningText || prev.warning     || 'None',                                                                                                    
      type: p.type || prev.type || printerConfig.type || '', 
      lastPrint: p.lastPrint !== undefined ? (p.lastPrint || null) : (prev.lastPrint || null),
      lastSeen:  p.lastSeen !== undefined ? (p.lastSeen || '') : (prev.lastSeen || '')                                                                                              
  };                                                                                                                                                          
  flow.set('dash_printers', rows);                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                           
  msg.payload = Object.values(rows).sort((a, b) => {
          const ai = order.indexOf(a.name);                                                                                                                
          const bi = order.indexOf(b.name);                                                                                                                
          return (ai === -1 ? 999 : ai) - (bi === -1 ? 999 : bi);                                                                                          
      });                                                                                                                                                  
   msg.action = 'replace';                                                                                                                                  
  return msg;

CREATE alarms
query:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS alarms (
    id           SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    source       VARCHAR(50),
    name         VARCHAR(100),
    equipment    VARCHAR(100),
    severity     VARCHAR(20),
    message      TEXT,
    alarm_code   INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    warning_code INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    active       BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    acked_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
    acked_by     VARCHAR(100),
    resolved_at  TIMESTAMPTZ
);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS alarms_equipment_active
    ON alarms (equipment) WHERE active = true;
